query:
INSERT INTO knowledge_base (
  compound_name,
  category,
  summary,
  mechanisms,
  clinical_evidence,
  dosing,
  contraindications,
  interactions,
  sources,
  tags,
  created_by
) VALUES (
  'Bempedoic Acid',
  'Cholesterol-Lowering Agent / PPARα Agonist',
  'Bempedoic acid is a dual-mechanism cholesterol-lowering drug that works through liver-specific ATP citrate lyase inhibition (pro-drug activation) and whole-body PPARα activation. Originally designed as a "smarter statin" to avoid muscle side effects, new research reveals it has powerful systemic anti-inflammatory effects independent of cholesterol reduction, making it valuable for cardiovascular risk reduction and metabolic health.',
  JSON_ARRAY(
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'ATP Citrate Lyase Inhibition (Liver-Specific)', 'description', 'Bempedoic acid is administered as a pro-drug that becomes active only in the liver via very long-chain acyl-CoA synthetase 1 (ACSVL1). Once activated, it inhibits ATP citrate lyase, an enzyme upstream of HMG-CoA reductase in the cholesterol synthesis pathway. This blocks cholesterol production specifically in hepatocytes, lowering LDL and ApoB levels without affecting muscle tissue.'),
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'PPARα Activation (Whole-Body)', 'description', 'In its inactive form, bempedoic acid directly binds to and activates PPARα receptors throughout the body (liver, heart, muscles, brain). PPARα is a master transcription factor that promotes fatty acid oxidation, reduces systemic inflammation, and protects against metabolic dysfunction. This mechanism operates independently of cholesterol lowering and explains the drug''s potent anti-inflammatory effects.'),
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'Systemic Anti-Inflammatory Effects', 'description', 'PPARα activation suppresses pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-α, IL-6, IL-1β) and reduces high-sensitivity C-reactive protein (hsCRP). Unlike statins, where inflammation reduction is secondary to cholesterol lowering, bempedoic acid''s anti-inflammatory effect is direct and substantial, contributing to cardiovascular protection beyond lipid management.'),
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'Metabolic Health Improvement', 'description', 'Through PPARα activation, bempedoic acid enhances fatty acid oxidation, improves insulin sensitivity, and supports mitochondrial function. This has implications for metabolic syndrome, type 2 diabetes, and neurodegenerative conditions like Alzheimer''s disease where metabolic dysfunction plays a role.')
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', '13% reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE) in high-risk patients', 'source', 'CLEAR Outcomes Trial'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Significant reduction in LDL cholesterol and ApoB levels comparable to moderate-intensity statins', 'source', 'Phase 3 clinical trials'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Substantial reduction in hsCRP (high-sensitivity C-reactive protein) exceeding what would be expected from cholesterol lowering alone', 'source', 'Clinical trial inflammatory marker analysis'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Lower incidence of muscle-related adverse events compared to statins due to liver-specific activation of cholesterol-lowering mechanism', 'source', 'Safety profile studies'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Dual-mechanism action confirmed: ATP citrate lyase inhibition (liver) + PPARα activation (whole body)', 'source', 'Cell Metabolism 2026 (upcoming publication)')
  ),
  JSON_OBJECT(
    'typical', '180 mg once daily',
    'range', '180 mg/day (standard dose)',
    'notes', 'Taken orally, with or without food. Pro-drug activation occurs in liver; PPARα activation occurs systemically in inactive form. No dose adjustment needed for mild-to-moderate renal impairment.'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Severe renal impairment (eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73 m²) - not recommended',
    'End-stage renal disease requiring dialysis',
    'Pregnancy and lactation (insufficient safety data)',
    'Known hypersensitivity to bempedoic acid or excipients'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Statins: Can be used in combination for additive LDL-lowering effect; monitor for muscle symptoms',
    'Simvastatin: Increases simvastatin exposure; limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day when co-administered',
    'Pravastatin: Increases pravastatin exposure; limit pravastatin to 40 mg/day when co-administered',
    'Warfarin and other anticoagulants: May potentiate effects; monitor INR closely',
    'Fibrates: Potential for additive PPARα activation; use with caution'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2215024 (CLEAR Outcomes Trial)',
    'https://www.cell.com/cell-metabolism/ (Cell Metabolism 2026 - PPARα discovery)',
    'https://www.fda.gov/drugs/drug-approvals-and-databases (FDA approval documentation)',
    'https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/ (ATP citrate lyase inhibition mechanism)'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Cholesterol Lowering',
    'PPARα Agonist',
    'Anti-inflammatory',
    'Cardiovascular Protection',
    'ATP Citrate Lyase Inhibitor',
    'LDL Reduction',
    'hsCRP Reduction',
    'Metabolic Health',
    'Statin Alternative',
    'Pro-drug',
    'Fatty Acid Oxidation',
    'Alzheimer Prevention'
  ),
  1
);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 34DkbYAaF6MBWwK.96fd8e3f3a28 --database 6XDPcfqtDX3GCRKvFSNyjg --execute INSERT INTO knowledge_base (

    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Lower incidence of muscle-related adverse events compared to statins due to liver-specific activation of cholesterol-lowering mechanism', 'source', 'Safety profile studies'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Dual-mechanism action confirmed: ATP citrate lyase inhibition (liver) + PPARα activation (whole body)', 'source', 'Cell Metabolism 2026 (upcoming publication)')

  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Severe renal impairment (eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73 m²) - not recommended',
    'End-stage renal disease requiring dialysis',
    'Pregnancy and lactation (insufficient safety data)',
    'Known hypersensitivity to bempedoic acid or excipients'

  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Statins: Can be used in combination for additive LDL-lowering effect; monitor for muscle symptoms',
    'Simvastatin: Increases simvastatin exposure; limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day when co-administered',
    'Pravastatin: Increases pravastatin exposure; limit pravastatin to 40 mg/day when co-administered',
    'Warfarin and other anticoagulants: May potentiate effects; monitor INR closely',
    'Fibrates: Potential for additive PPARα activation; use with caution'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2215024 (CLEAR Outcomes Trial)',
    'https://www.cell.com/cell-metabolism/ (Cell Metabolism 2026 - PPARα discovery)',
    'https://www.fda.gov/drugs/drug-approvals-and-databases (FDA approval documentation)',

  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Cholesterol Lowering',
    'PPARα Agonist',
    'Anti-inflammatory',
    'Cardiovascular Protection',
    'ATP Citrate Lyase Inhibitor',
    'LDL Reduction',
    'hsCRP Reduction',
    'Metabolic Health',
    'Statin Alternative',
